content:
{
  "title": "Fact-Check-Aware Prompting: Integrating Real-Time Verification Signals into LLM Inputs",
  "problem": "Large language models frequently produce hallucinated or factually incorrect statements, especially when dealing with knowledge-intensive queries. Existing prompting strategies are insufficient to reliably mitigate these errors.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are increasingly deployed in critical domains, ensuring high factual accuracy is essential. Current approaches like chain-of-thought or retrieval augmentation reduce, but do not eliminate, hallucinations. Incorporating explicit, automated fact-checking cues at prompt time could directly steer model outputs toward verifiable content.",
  "method": "We propose a 'Fact-Check-Aware Prompting' method, where initial model outputs or user queries are passed through a lightweight, automated fact-checking module (e.g., based on retrieval or contradiction detection against a trusted corpus). The resulting verification signals—such as 'VERIFIED', 'CONTRADICTS EVIDENCE', or 'NO MATCH FOUND'—are injected as structured tokens directly into the next-stage prompt. The LLM is thus guided to revise, justify, or refrain from uncertain claims. This process can be iteratively applied, allowing the model to condition on external factuality assessments at each generation step.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate this method on QA and open-ended generation benchmarks with strong factuality annotations (e.g., TruthfulQA, FEVER, and WizardLM-Eval). Baselines include standard prompting, retrieval-augmented prompting, and chain-of-thought prompting. Factuality will be measured via human annotation and automated tools (e.g., GPT-4-based fact-grading), while fluency and completeness are assessed to ensure overall response quality."
}
